# The sesam-secret merge driver decrypts the three sides of a secret and does a
# 3-way text merge. When both branches change disjoint regions the merge is
# clean: git merge succeeds and the revealed plaintext carries both edits with
# no conflict markers.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

# Stash the three content versions outside the worktree: sesam's hooks run an
# aggressive clean that wipes untracked files under the sesam dir (the worktree
# root here), which would otherwise delete these helpers before we use them.
cp base.txt $TMPDIR/base.txt
cp ours.txt $TMPDIR/ours.txt
cp theirs.txt $TMPDIR/theirs.txt

# Init sesam and install the merge driver git-config (the .gitattributes
# mapping secrets to merge=sesam-merge-secret is always written by init).
exec sesam init --user admin --install-merge
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# Base version XB of the secret, sealed and committed on the default branch.
cp $TMPDIR/base.txt secret.txt
exec sesam add secret.txt --group admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'add secret (XB)'

# Branch OURS changes only the first line.
exec git checkout -b ours
cp $TMPDIR/ours.txt secret.txt
exec sesam seal
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'ours: edit first line'

# Branch THEIRS branches off the XB commit (ours~1) and changes only the last line.
exec git checkout -b theirs ours~1
cp $TMPDIR/theirs.txt secret.txt
exec sesam seal
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'theirs: edit last line'

# Merge THEIRS into OURS. The edits are disjoint, so the merge is clean and git
# merge exits zero.
exec git checkout ours
exec git merge theirs

# The revealed plaintext holds both edits and no conflict markers.
grep 'first = OURS' secret.txt
grep 'last = THEIRS' secret.txt
grep 'middle = BASE' secret.txt
! grep '^<<<<<<< ' secret.txt
! grep '^>>>>>>> ' secret.txt

-- base.txt --
first = BASE
middle = BASE
last = BASE

-- ours.txt --
first = OURS
middle = BASE
last = BASE

-- theirs.txt --
first = BASE
middle = BASE
last = THEIRS
